items.briefing:
The Privacy Commissioner has found that Optus breached privacy obligations by listing customers in White Pages without proper consent — a reminder that even large Australian telcos can mishandle your personal information. Separately, a new Bitdefender report warns that scams are now hitting Australians across every channel — email, SMS, phone calls, and social media — making it harder to spot a fake. State-sponsored groups from China and North Korea are also continuing to target Asia-Pacific organisations, meaning Australian businesses should remain alert.

**Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected message on any platform asking you to click a link or provide personal details, verify it directly through the organisation's official website or phone number before responding.

featured.title: Privacy Commissioner finds Optus breached privacy in White Pages listing matter
featured.quote: Australia's privacy regulator found Optus violated citizen privacy in major enforcement action.
